message.content: Adversaries may stage capabilities by uploading, installing, or setting up tools and resources needed for their operations. This involves placing developed or obtained capabilities on infrastructure they control, which could be purchased, rented, or compromised. Common staging locations include web services like GitHub or Pastebin, and Platform-as-a-Service (PaaS) offerings. Staging capabilities can facilitate various malicious activities, such as initial access and post-compromise actions. For example, Mustang Panda has used controlled servers to validate tracking pixels sent to phishing victims. Mitigation strategies include monitoring and restricting access to known staging platforms, employing network segmentation, and using threat intelligence to identify and block adversary infrastructure. Regularly updating security policies and conducting thorough audits can also help in detecting and preventing the staging of malicious capabilities.
